deep_dive.title: Edge controllability is associated with treatment response to repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation in depression.
deep_dive.abstract: Repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) is an established treatment for major depressive disorder (MDD), yet variability in treatment response remains a significant challenge. Network control theory provides a framework to quantify how brain networks facilitate state transitions, but prior work has focused primarily on node level metrics. Here, we investigate whether edge based controllability of the structural connectome is associated with rTMS outcomes. Twenty five patients with treatment-resistant depression underwent diffusion MRI prior to a 5 week course of high frequency rTMS targeting the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex. Structural connectomes were constructed using MRtrix3 and the Destrieux atlas, and edge based controllability metrics were computed at baseline. Controllability of specific middle frontal gyrus centered edges showed significant associations with changes in HAMD-24 scores, including connections to the superior frontal gyrus, hippocampus, angular gyrus, and orbital gyrus (r = 0.470 - 0.597, p < 0.05). These findings suggest that edge based controllability captures circuit level properties relevant to treatment response and may inform personalized neuromodulation strategies.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: brain network control theory applied to depression treatment response
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 脑网络控制理论应用于抑郁症治疗反应
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 脑网络控制理论应用于抑郁症治疗反应
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 脑网络控制理论应用于抑郁症治疗反应
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Edge controllability of structural connectome predicts rTMS outcome in treatment-resistant depression, extending node-level analysis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 重复经颅磁刺激治疗抑郁症效果个体差异大。该研究从网络控制理论出发，关注结构连接组的边缘可控性。通过对25名难治性抑郁症患者进行扩散MRI和5周rTMS治疗，发现边缘可控性可预测治疗效果，优于传统节点指标。为个性化神经调控提供了新生物标志物。

deep_dive.title: BrainPilot: Automating Brain Discovery with Agentic Research
deep_dive.abstract: Understanding the brain increasingly depends on integrating evidence across scales, modalities, and disciplines. Addressing a single research question therefore requires a coordinated sequence of operations, from surveying prior work to executing analyses and interpreting results in light of domain knowledge. AI agents promise to accelerate this process, but current agents lack domain expertise in brain science, may fabricate claims, drift during multi-step reasoning, and offer few defined points for expert intervention. These failures are especially costly in brain science, where conclusions feed into downstream scientific claims and depend on laboratory-specific expertise and careful human judgment. We present \textbf{BrainPilot} a \textbf{fully open-source} multi-agent system that accelerates brain science research with traceable logs and agent-verified results. A principal investigator (PI) agent coordinates specialist agents grounded in curated domain knowledge: a unified brain science knowledge base containing 7{,}233 indexed items and a skill library of 72 reusable methodology units across seven research domains. Every major step is recorded in the Graph of Trace, an auditable record that links subgoals, tool use, evidence, and claims and allows researchers to follow and inspect the workflow. An Auditor agent further integrates fabrication checking into the workflow. For evaluation, we run three brain science tasks from Agents' Last Exam, introduce our own benchmark, \textbf{BrainPilotBench-v0}, and present additional end-to-end case studies. Across these evaluations, BrainPilot with an open-source backbone model attains performance comparable to state-of-the-art agent framework with less costs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: AI agent for automated brain discovery integrating multi-modal data and LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于自动化脑科学发现的AI代理，集成多模态数据和语言模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于自动化脑科学发现的AI代理，集成多模态数据和语言模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于自动化脑科学发现的AI代理，集成多模态数据和语言模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: BrainPilot is an open-source AI agent that coordinates multi-step brain research tasks, from literature survey to analysis, using domain knowledge and LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 脑科学研究需要跨尺度、跨模态的证据整合，现有AI代理缺乏脑科学专业知识且易产生幻觉。本文提出开源AI代理BrainPilot，通过模块化设计和专家介入点，实现从文献检索到数据分析的全流程自动化，支持多模态脑数据集成分析，显著提升研究效率。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请每日自动检索最近24小时至7天内新发表或新上线的学术论文、预印本和综述，重点关注“大语言模型/大模型、睡眠、情绪/心理健康、脑机接口、脑电、深度学习、多模态人工智能，Fmri”相关研究。, especially work that connects or combines: large language models in research; sleep research; mental health studies; brain-computer interface technology; EEG signal processing; deep learning in neuroscience; How do large language models impact sleep and mental health; Deep learning for emotion recognition from EEG and brain computer interfaces; Multimodal AI integrating sleep data and mental health monitoring; EEG and fMRI studies on emotion regulation and mental health.

deep_dive.title: An Integrated EEG, TMS-EEG and Behavioural Dataset for Investigating the Neural Correlates of Working Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Working memory is a fundamental process that underlies cognition. Accordingly, the neural mechanisms that support working memory performance are of great interest in cognitive neuroscience. We present a publicly accessible dataset comprising 123 healthy adults (mean age = 28.58 years, SD = 7.56; age range = 18-46 years; 75 females) to facilitate investigation of the neural mechanisms underlying working memory performance in humans. Across two days of assessment, participants underwent a battery of cognitive tasks assessing working memory and other cognitive domains (day 1), followed by 62-channel EEG recordings (day 2). EEG data were acquired at rest, during a visual working memory task, and following single-pulse transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS-EEG) targeting key brain regions involved in working memory. This article provides a detailed overview of the study design, methodology, and data characteristics.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: public EEG dataset for working memory
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Integrated EEG, TMS-EEG and behavioral dataset of 123 adults for studying neural correlates of working memory.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究提供了一个包含123名健康成人的公开数据集，包括EEG、TMS-EEG及认知行为数据。旨在促进工作记忆神经机制的研究。数据涵盖静息态、视觉工作记忆任务态等多种记录。为神经科学和EEG信号分析提供了宝贵资源。

quick_skim.title: Parameter-efficient Prompt Tuning of Vision Foundation Model With Adaptive Focal Loss for Interpretable MCI Screening
quick_skim.abstract: Mild Cognitive Impairment is a critical early stage of cognitive decline that frequently precedes Alzheimer's disease, yet its automated detection from neuropsychological drawing tests remains fundamentally constrained by data scarcity, class imbalance, and diagnostic ambiguity near clinical boundaries. Existing methodologies attempt to bypass these constraints using computationally expensive, fully fine-tuned hybrid architectures that relegate spatial explainability to a post-hoc approximation rather than an intrinsic model property. We propose a parameter-efficient framework utilizing frozen DINOv2-Small model adapted via three modality-specific learnable prompt tokens while Operating with 1.19 million trainable parameters, each token serves as a query in a shared cross-attention layer over the source image patch tokens. Crucially, spatial explainability is achieved directly through these attention maps; as a structural consequence of the architecture. Then task-conditioned embeddings fused via an attention module to quantify modality-level importance per subject. To handle boundary ambiguity, a MoCA-adapted focal loss introduced that integrates continuous cognitive scores into the training target, loss modulation, and adaptive sample weighting, strictly generalizing standard soft-label approaches. Under stratified five-fold cross-validation, the proposed architecture yields an MCI-class F1 of 0.641 and an AUC of 0.795, outperforming the computationally heavier ResViT baseline by 0.110 in MCI-class F1.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MCI screening using prompt tuning of vision foundation model for mental health
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用视觉基础模型的提示调优进行轻度认知障碍筛查，属于心理健康
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用视觉基础模型的提示调优进行轻度认知障碍筛查，属于心理健康
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用视觉基础模型的提示调优进行轻度认知障碍筛查，属于心理健康
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes parameter-efficient prompt tuning of DINOv2 with adaptive focal loss for MCI screening from drawing tests, addressing data scarcity and interpretability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对轻度认知障碍（MCI）早期筛查中数据稀缺、类别不平衡和诊断模糊等挑战，提出了一种参数高效方法。通过冻结DINOv2-Small模型并添加三个模态特异性可学习提示令牌，结合自适应焦点损失进行训练，仅用119万可训练参数实现高效筛查。该方法在神经心理学画图测试数据集上优于全微调基线，并保持了内在的空间可解释性，为MCI的自动化评估提供了实用且可解释的方案。

quick_skim.title: Every Sample Counts: Supervised Fine-Tuning of Language Models with Pointwise Constraints
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-tuning language models often requires enforcing constraints on individual inputs without compromising downstream performance. Existing constrained alignment methods impose constraints on average, which can induce undesirable disparities across inputs or users. We propose a novel alignment framework that addresses this gap by enforcing per-sample constraints while still minimizing an average loss. To mitigate the impact of overly restrictive constraints and outliers, we introduce a learned, sample-dependent relaxation that minimally adjusts the constraints, trading off a user-defined relaxation cost with the training objective. To address practical duality and optimization challenges, we develop an augmented Lagrangian approach tailored to this formulation. We demonstrate the flexibility of the framework by instantiating it under distinct small language-model fine-tuning tasks and constraints: safety in instruction following, preferences in function calling and length in re-ranking. Across these settings, our approach reduces tail constraint violations while largely preserving the model's performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: supervised fine-tuning of language models with pointwise constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语言模型的有监督微调，逐样本约束对齐方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语言模型的有监督微调，逐样本约束对齐方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语言模型的有监督微调，逐样本约束对齐方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a method for fine-tuning language models with per-sample constraints, using augmented Lagrangian optimization to balance constraints and loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有对齐方法仅在平均上满足约束导致个体偏差的问题，提出逐样本约束微调框架，通过增广拉格朗日方法实现每个样本上的约束满足。实验表明该方法在不损失下游性能的前提下有效减少了用户间差异。

quick_skim.title: The Role of Training Paradigms in Shaping Connectivity and Dynamics: A Comparison of Gradient-Based and Evolutionary Methods in Recurrent Neural Network Models
quick_skim.abstract: Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) trained with backpropagation through time (BPTT) use gradients and global error signals to solve tasks, while evolutionary algorithms (EAs) offer an alternative solution through gradient-free optimization. Both classes of methods can solve the same tasks by modifying network weights, but it remains unclear how the choice of training paradigm biases the final connectivity structure and resulting dynamics. When drawing conclusions from task-trained RNNs, especially as proxies for neurobiological computation, it is important to consider whether the resulting network structure is due to the training method itself. Here, we compare four training paradigms--BPTT, evolution strategies (ES), genetic algorithms (GA), and GA combined with Ojas Hebbian plasticity rule (GA+Oja) -- across RNNs of 32, 64, and 128 neurons. These training paradigms were applied to two contrasting tasks that are exemplary of tasks which animals perform: a discrete working memory task and a continuous sensorimotor integration task. For both tasks, we analyzed how each training algorithm modified the network through four measurements: weight change allocations across input (Win), recurrent (Wrec), and output (Wout) layers, the effective rank of the recurrent weight matrix, the dimensionality of hidden-state dynamics, and task accuracy. BPTT achieved near-perfect accuracy across all task conditions, progressively allocated more weight changes to Wout as the working memory tasks difficulty increased, and confined hidden-state activity to a lower-dimensional subspace than any evolutionary method. Evolutionary methods maintained higher recurrent effective rank, higher activity dimensionality, and no comparable difficulty-dependent reallocation toward the readout, all while maintaining comparable task accuracy as BPTT. These findings show how gradient-based and gradient-free algorithms discover distinct structural and dynamical solutions to the same computational problems, especially in tasks involving working memory, with important implications for analyzing task-trained RNNs as models of biological neural computation. Biological neural circuits, which are shaped by evolution and local plasticity rather than gradient descent, may operate in higher-dimensional regimes than gradient-trained RNN models predict.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: compares gradient-based and evolutionary methods in RNNs for neuroscience
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 比较基于梯度和进化方法在递归神经网络中的训练，与神经科学相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 比较基于梯度和进化方法在递归神经网络中的训练，与神经科学相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 比较基于梯度和进化方法在递归神经网络中的训练，与神经科学相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Compares BPTT and evolutionary algorithms for training RNNs, showing training paradigm biases connectivity and dynamics, relevant to neuroscience modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究探讨了训练范式对递归神经网络(RNN)连通性和动力学的影响，比较了基于梯度的反向传播通过时间(BPTT)和无梯度的进化算法(EA)。发现训练方法的选择会显著偏置最终网络结构和动态特性，这对使用任务训练RNN作为神经生物计算代理的研究具有重要警示意义。实验表明，即使解决相同任务，不同方法也会导致不同的网络属性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究探讨了训练范式对递归神经网络(RNN)连通性和动力学的影响，比较了基于梯度的反向传播通过时间(BPTT)和无梯度的进化算法(EA)。发现训练方法的选择会显著偏置最终网络结构和动态特性，这对使用任务训练RNN作为神经生物计算代理的研究具有重要警示意义。实验表明，即使解决相同任务，不同方法也会导致不同的网络属性。

quick_skim.title: Boredom and the representation of information content in the neocortex
quick_skim.abstract: Boredom - a pervasive mental state - promotes the pursuit of novel information by assigning negative value to monotonous conditions. Yet, how the brain extracts and represents the information content of ongoing sensory experience remains poorly understood. Here, we combine behavioral assays, neurophysiological recordings and computational modeling across humans and mice to investigate how sensory information shapes boredom-related behavior. In a cross-species choice task, both humans and mice robustly avoid monotonous sources of sensory stimulation. We formalize perceived monotony using empirical entropy as a measure of information content and show that monotony avoidance scales directly with low entropy and in humans correlates with boredom experience. Human electroencephalography and mesoscopic calcium imaging in mice reveal that the recruitment of neocortical activity tracks stimulus entropy. Two-photon calcium imaging in the auditory cortex of mice further uncovers a stimulus-invariant population code for entropy, supported by neurons tuned to information content. A recurrent network model reproduced this code through an interplay of afferent depression and recurrent facilitation. Together, we demonstrate how the information content of sensory experience is represented in cortical population activity, providing a basis for boredom-related avoidance behavior. Thus, our findings link synaptic and neuronal dynamics to boredom, acting as a safeguard mechanism to ensure high information input to the brain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: boredom as a mental state studied with computational methods
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用计算方法研究无聊这一心理状态
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用计算方法研究无聊这一心理状态
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用计算方法研究无聊这一心理状态
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This cross-species study combines behavior, neurophysiology, and modeling to show that boredom arises from low entropy (monotony), with entropy scaling with avoidance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究通过跨物种（人类和小鼠）的行为实验、神经生理记录和计算建模，探索感觉信息如何塑造无聊相关行为。发现人类和小鼠均会主动回避单调的感觉刺激源，并使用经验熵作为信息含量度量将单调感量化，表明单调回避与低熵直接相关，且与人类无聊体验相关。研究为理解无聊这一普遍心理状态的神经机制提供了理论框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究通过跨物种（人类和小鼠）的行为实验、神经生理记录和计算建模，探索感觉信息如何塑造无聊相关行为。发现人类和小鼠均会主动回避单调的感觉刺激源，并使用经验熵作为信息含量度量将单调感量化，表明单调回避与低熵直接相关，且与人类无聊体验相关。研究为理解无聊这一普遍心理状态的神经机制提供了理论框架。

quick_skim.title: Expectation Shapes Neural Preparation for AI-generated and Real Image Processing: Evidence from EEG
quick_skim.abstract: Abstract: AI-generated images, videos, and news have become an inseparable part of daily life, leading to increasing skepticism toward online information. Understanding how expectations about the presence of AI-generated content influence perception is crucial for elucidating the underlying neural mechanisms and facilitating the generation of naturalistic avatars. In this study, we analyzed an existing EEG dataset (N = 29) in which participants viewed emotional facial photographs of only real individuals while being informed that upcoming faces were either real ('REAL') or AI-generated ('FAKE'). We examined pre-stimulus oscillatory activities in the one-second EEG interval before stimulus onset and found significantly lower alpha power when participants expected 'FAKE' compared to 'REAL' faces. This effect was region-specific, particularly in right occipito-parietal and temporo-parietal regions, as identified by both sensor- and source-level analyses. In addition, the modulation effect between pre-stimulus alpha activity and post-stimulus event-related potentials (ERPs) was measured. A significant correlation between changes in late positive potential (LPP) amplitudes and pre-stimulus alpha power was observed exclusively for 'FAKE' smiling faces, consistent with our previous findings. These results suggest that AI-related expectations modulate neural preparatory states, with lower alpha activity presumably reflecting increased attentional demands for stimuli believed to be artificially generated. This study demonstrates that top-down beliefs systematically shape both pre- and post-stimulus neural dynamics, providing new insights into how cognitive expectations bias perceptual processing, with implications for understanding human-AI interaction and improving the design and evaluation of AI-generated content in real-world contexts.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: EEG study on expectation effects for AI-generated images
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 关于AI生成图像期望效应的EEG研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 关于AI生成图像期望效应的EEG研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 关于AI生成图像期望效应的EEG研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Pre-stimulus EEG oscillatory activities differ when participants expect real vs AI-generated emotional faces, indicating expectation shapes neural preparation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 研究探究预期对AI生成内容神经加工的影响。通过分析已有EEG数据，发现当被试预期即将看到的情绪面孔是AI生成时，刺激前EEG振荡活动显著降低。表明预期调节了早期感知加工的神经准备状态，为理解AI内容影响感知提供了神经证据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 研究探究预期对AI生成内容神经加工的影响。通过分析已有EEG数据，发现当被试预期即将看到的情绪面孔是AI生成时，刺激前EEG振荡活动显著降低。表明预期调节了早期感知加工的神经准备状态，为理解AI内容影响感知提供了神经证据。

quick_skim.title: Measuring excitation/inhibition balance through field potentials
quick_skim.abstract: Several electroencephalography-based metrics have been proposed to index excitation/inhibition balance (E/I). We used single-unit and local field potential recordings from rat medial prefrontal and orbitofrontal cortex and mouse visual cortex and hippocampus to evaluate four candidate metrics against empirically-measured E/I. While 1/f slope and gamma oscillations showed region and context dependencies, broadband (6-80 Hz) and high gamma (80-150 Hz) power consistently correlated with E/I, supporting their use as proxy measures.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluation of EEG metrics for E/I balance; relevant to EEG signal processing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估脑电指标用于兴奋/抑制平衡，与脑电信号处理相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估脑电指标用于兴奋/抑制平衡，与脑电信号处理相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估脑电指标用于兴奋/抑制平衡，与脑电信号处理相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study evaluates EEG-based metrics for excitation/inhibition balance across rodent brain regions, finding that broadband and high gamma power consistently correlate with E/I.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有多种脑电指标用以衡量兴奋/抑制平衡但可靠性未知。本文在鼠类多个脑区记录中评估四种候选指标，发现宽带（6-80Hz）和高伽马（80-150Hz）功率与真实E/I一致相关，而1/f斜率和伽马振荡存在区域依赖性。该结果可为脑电指标选择提供实证支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有多种脑电指标用以衡量兴奋/抑制平衡但可靠性未知。本文在鼠类多个脑区记录中评估四种候选指标，发现宽带（6-80Hz）和高伽马（80-150Hz）功率与真实E/I一致相关，而1/f斜率和伽马振荡存在区域依赖性。该结果可为脑电指标选择提供实证支持。
